input_candidates.truth_value: This study proposes a data-driven framework for yield strength prediction in medium-manganese low-density steels by integrating contributions from four strengthening mechanisms (solid solution, grain refinement, dislocation, Orowan), using Gaussian Mixture Model data augmentation and Genetic Algorithm-Extremely Randomized Trees, validated by experiments.
input_candidates.prediction_value: Development and experimental validation of a data-driven yield strength prediction framework for medium-manganese low-density steels, integrating Gaussian Mixture Model data augmentation, Extra Trees ensemble learning, Genetic Algorithm hyperparameter optimization, and SHAP interpretability, verified on Fe-0.25C-3Mn-2Al-0.4Si steel annealed at 750, 800, and 850 degrees Celsius.

input_candidates.truth_value: The GA-Extra Trees model predicted YS for the experimental steels with errors of 12 MPa (at 750°C), 10 MPa (800°C), and 19 MPa (850°C), while the standard Extra Trees gave 34, 31, 36 MPa differences.
input_candidates.prediction_value: Original dataset of 177 entries was expanded to 776 using Gaussian Mixture Model (GMM) data augmentation based on Bayesian Information Criterion (BIC) and Expectation Maximization (EM) algorithm.

input_candidates.truth_value: Literature validation on 6 data points gave average prediction error 1.84% (vs. 1.56% for own experiments), demonstrating good generalization.
input_candidates.prediction_value: Extra Trees model test set performance before optimization: RMSE=76.2 MPa, MAPE=9.8%, R²=0.978. After GA optimization: RMSE=31.60 MPa, MAPE=4.39%, R²=0.997.

input_candidates.truth_value: Fig. 12 engineering stress-strain curves show yield strengths around 709, 968, 964 MPa for ANT750, ANT800, ANT850 respectively, but no UTS values reported.
input_candidates.prediction_value: GA-optimized Extra Trees hyperparameters (Table 2): n_estimators=1657, max_depth=51, min_samples_split=3, min_samples_leaf=1.0, max_features=0.85, max_samples=0.92.

input_candidates.truth_value: Fig. 13 SEM morphology images (not accessible) illustrate ferrite and bainite grains; quantitative data from Image-Pro Plus given in text.
input_candidates.prediction_value: Comparison with alternative optimizations: Grid Search-ET RMSE=80.239 MPa, Bayesian-ET RMSE=80.721 MPa, versus GA-ET RMSE=31.60 MPa.

input_candidates.truth_value: Fig. 14 EBSD-based GND density distributions and Taylor factor histograms shown but numeric curves not readable.
input_candidates.prediction_value: Prediction error reduction via GA optimization for experimental samples: 750 degrees C reduced by 22 MPa (Extra Trees diff 34 MPa to GA-ET diff 12 MPa); 800 degrees C reduced by 21 MPa (31 to 10 MPa); 850 degrees C reduced by 17 MPa (36 to 19 MPa).

input_candidates.truth_value: The model does not fuse microstructural image information, limiting deeper mining of structure-property relationships; future work may employ deep learning on multimodal data.
input_candidates.prediction_value: Theoretical strengthening contributions and calculated vs actual YS (Table 3): AN750/ANT750 sigma0=220, sigmag=264, sigmad=263, sigmaOrowan=0 MPa, calculated YS=747 MPa; AN800/ANT800 sigma0=220, sigmag=256, sigmad=459, sigmaOrowan=0 MPa, calculated=935 MPa; AN850/ANT850 sigma0=220, sigmag=292, sigmad=414, sigmaOrowan=0 MPa, calculated=926 MPa.

input_candidates.truth_value: The Orowan contribution for the experimental steel is 0 MPa due to lack of precipitates; no significant precipitation strengthening observed.
